Now with looping, your goal essentially is to push the prospect down the straight line from the open to the close. And as you're doing that, you're taking someone from what? A state of relative uncertainty about the three tens to a state of absolute certainty about the three tens. And remember, the order in which you establish the three tens is as follows. Well one, product first, right? You first want to sell them on the product, they get them hooked into the product. Number two, you want to sell them on you so they trust and connect with you. Then number three, you want to essentially get them hooked into the company so they trust and connect with the company, right? And I use that word hooked into not in a negative way. Just I want you to understand that it's more than just logic here. See in order to truly operate at the highest level of the straight line, we're not just dealing with logic. We're dealing with both logic and emotion. So the idea is this, is an order for everything. Now with the three tens, the order is as follows. I'll repeat it one more time. One, product, you want to sell them on the product. Two, you want to get them to trust and connect with you. Three, you want them to trust and connect with the company. Now in terms of logic and emotion, the order is this. You want to create a logical case first, which then frees them up to be moved emotionally, which comes after the fact. So logic first, emotion second. And remember, people do not buy on logic. They buy on emotion and they justify with logic. But one without the other and the system does not work very well. You see, you'll never close at the highest level if you're just going after logic or emotion. If you go purely on logic, it's very hard to get people to say yes. That's the bottom line. And if you only go on emotion, you might get someone to say yes, but the next morning they wake up and you know what they get? They get buyer's remorse because that voice in the back of their head, that logical voice is saying, wait a second, this doesn't add up. I don't really know. I'm not that certain. So the idea is this. We use this airtight logical case to quiet the voice in the back of their head that says, don't do it. And once that voice is working for you, saying, wow, this makes a lot of sense, then we switch to the emotional case, which is really about this need they have and how they see themselves in the future using your proctin feeling. That's the emotional side. So we use logic first that frees them up to be moved emotionally and bam, you start getting to that point where you can close everyone who's closeable.
